## Opening image
<!-- vo-meta: {"section_id": "opening-image", "voice_id": "JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb", "model": "eleven_v3", "settings": {"stability": "creative"}, "notes": "George's warm, captivating storyteller quality handles this hushed, conspiratorial opening perfectly — the [quiet] tag lands naturally in his resonant register, and the prior LIKED take confirms he handles [pause] with real weight."} -->

[quiet] There's a tab open on your browser right now that you haven't looked at in three days. [pause] You know the one.

## The problem we don't name
<!-- vo-meta: {"section_id": "the-problem-we-don-t-name", "voice_id": "JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb", "model": "eleven_v3", "settings": {"stability": "creative"}, "notes": "Keeping George across all sections for narrative cohesion; the [reflective] tag suits his mature warmth, and the added [thoughtful] before the closing line sharpens the gentle challenge without breaking the meditative tone."} -->

[reflective] We talk about focus like it's a skill you either have or you don't. Like some people just — arrive at their desk and the world goes still. [pause] But that's not what focus is. [thoughtful] That's not even close.

## What focus actually is
<!-- vo-meta: {"section_id": "what-focus-actually-is", "voice_id": "JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb", "model": "eleven_v3", "settings": {"stability": "creative"}, "notes": "This is the script's intellectual spine — energy should rise here. The [building] tag lifts George's delivery out of the reflective mode, and [earnest] on the final couplet lands the reframe with conviction before the tone softens again."} -->

[building] Focus isn't the absence of distraction. It's a decision you make about what deserves your attention — and then [pause] you make it again. And again. Every few minutes, sometimes every few seconds. [earnest] It's not a state. It's a practice.

## The calm underneath
<!-- vo-meta: {"section_id": "the-calm-underneath", "voice_id": "JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb", "model": "eleven_v3", "settings": {"stability": "creative"}, "notes": "After the rising energy of the previous section, this one calls for a deliberate settling — [warm] into [gentle] mirrors the very calm being described, and George's natural resonance carries the philosophical weight without effort."} -->

[warm] And here's the part that took me a long time to understand. Calm isn't what you feel when everything is handled. [pause] Calm is what makes it possible to handle anything. [gentle] It comes first. You have to build it before you need it.

## A different way to work
<!-- vo-meta: {"section_id": "a-different-way-to-work", "voice_id": "JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb", "model": "eleven_v3", "settings": {"stability": "creative"}, "notes": "The [sighs] here is doing real emotional work — it's the exhale of someone who has lived this truth, not just stated it. Per USER FACT on matching tags to voice character, a sighing, knowing quality sits squarely in George's mature storyteller range."} -->

[slow, deliberate] So what does that look like? Fewer inputs. Longer stretches of one thing. Letting a thought finish before you hand it off to someone else — or to your future self. [pause] It feels inefficient at first. [sighs] It isn't.

## What you get back
<!-- vo-meta: {"section_id": "what-you-get-back", "voice_id": "JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb", "model": "eleven_v3", "settings": {"stability": "creative"}, "notes": "This is the emotional payoff of the whole script — the arc peaks here. Moving from [reflective] into [warm] and finally [quietly moved] mirrors the listener's journey and gives George room to colour the closing line with genuine feeling rather than polish."} -->

[reflective] When you stop fragmenting your attention, something shifts. The work gets better — obviously. But more than that, [warm] you start to feel like the person doing the work. Present. Capable. [quietly moved] Like your mind is actually yours again.

## Close
<!-- vo-meta: {"section_id": "close", "voice_id": "JBFqnCBsd6RMkjVDRZzb", "model": "eleven_v3", "settings": {"stability": "creative"}, "notes": "The script closes exactly as it opened — [quiet], intimate, unhurried. Bookending with the same tag creates formal symmetry and signals to George's delivery that this is a whispered confidence rather than a grand conclusion; the two [pause] beats let each short sentence land as a separate thought."} -->

[quiet] You don't need more hours. [pause] You need fewer things competing for the ones you already have. [pause] Start there.
